schema.appName: FIRE Number Calculator India — Financial Independence
schema.appDescription: Calculate your FIRE number — the corpus needed for financial independence. Based on your monthly expenses, withdrawal rate, and inflation.

inputs.label: Safe Withdrawal Rate (%)
inputs.hint: 3–4% is generally safe for Indian conditions

howToSteps.name: Enter your details
howToSteps.text: Fill in the input fields for the FIRE Number Calculator.

howToSteps.name: View instant results
howToSteps.text: Results update in real time as you type.

howToSteps.name: Explore breakdown
howToSteps.text: Open the year-by-year breakdown to see numbers over time.

howToSteps.name: Compare scenarios
howToSteps.text: Adjust inputs to compare scenarios and optimise your decision.

faq.q: What is FIRE and how does it work?
faq.a: FIRE (Financial Independence, Retire Early) is about accumulating enough corpus that investment returns exceed living expenses — allowing you to stop working permanently. The FIRE number = annual expenses ÷ safe withdrawal rate. At 4% SWR: corpus = 25× annual expenses. Invest aggressively early, retire when corpus is reached.

faq.q: Is the 4% rule safe for Indian FIRE?
faq.a: The 4% rule was derived from US market data. For India: higher inflation (6% vs 2% in US), shorter equity market history, potential political/regulatory risk. 3–3.5% SWR is safer for India. At 3%: FIRE number = 33× annual expenses — you need 33% more corpus.

faq.q: What are the main FIRE variations?
faq.a: Lean FIRE: aggressive frugality, low expenses, small corpus. Fat FIRE: comfortable lifestyle, large corpus. Barista FIRE: semi-retirement with part-time work covering some expenses. Coast FIRE: invest early, coast to retirement on existing corpus without new investments. Choose based on your lifestyle goals.
No. Calculations run in your browser.
Yes. Works on all modern smartphones.

examples.heading: ₹80,000/month expenses — FIRE at 45
examples.text: At 6% inflation, expenses at 45 (13 years from age 32): ₹1,70,000/month. Annual: ₹20.4 lakh. FIRE number at 3.5% SWR: ₹20.4 lakh ÷ 0.035 = ₹5.83 crore. Monthly investment at 12% for 13 years: ₹1,82,000/month. Aggressive saving required.

examples.heading: Lean FIRE with ₹40,000/month lifestyle
examples.text: Lean FIRE at ₹40,000/month, targeting 45 (inflation-adjusted: ₹85,000/month). FIRE number: ₹85,000 × 12 / 0.04 = ₹2.55 crore. More achievable: ₹75,000/month investment at 12% for 13 years. Lean FIRE dramatically lowers the target.
